steps: A 2.4% annual increase means each year the house is worth 100% + 2.4% = 102.4% of the prior year's value.
Convert 102.4% to a decimal growth factor: $1.024$.
Starting value is \$174,000 and the factor compounds for $x$ years: $f(x) = 174{,}000(1.024)^x$.
eliminationHtml:
• Choice A — '$f(x)=174{,}000(0.024)^{x}$': Growth factor 0.024 would reduce the value to nearly zero each year — models a 97.6% annual loss. ✗
  • Choice B — '$f(x)=174{,}000(0.24)^{x}$': Growth factor 0.24 models a 76% annual loss, not a gain. ✗
  • Choice C — '$f(x)=174{,}000(1.024)^{x}$': Factor $1 + 0.024 = 1.024$ correctly represents a 2.4% annual increase. ✓
  • Choice D — '$f(x)=174{,}000(1.24)^{x}$': Factor 1.24 represents a 24% annual increase, ten times the stated rate. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
